negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap less than 1x annual contract value
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Cap below one year of fees provides insufficient protection for meaningful claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SaaS: 12 months fees. Enterprise: 1-2x annual fees. Outsourcing: 1-3x.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with 12-month minimum cap with super-cap carve-outs for data breach and IP infringement.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No liability cap or unlimited liability requested
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unlimited liability exposes provider to existential financial risk disproportionate to contract value.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: All markets: liability caps are standard practice. Unlimited liability is never market standard.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose cap at 12-24 months of annual fees with super-cap carve-outs at 2-5x for critical risks.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual exclusion of consequential damages without any carve-outs
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Blanket consequential damages exclusion eliminates most meaningful remedies for data breach, IP infringement.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard: exclude consequential damages generally but carve out data breach, IP, and confidentiality breach.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept mutual exclusion but add carve-outs for: data breach, IP infringement, confidentiality breach, and wilful misconduct.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap at 12+ months fees with 2-5x super-cap for key risks
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: This structure provides meaningful protection while managing provider risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Consistent with current market standard for enterprise SaaS and IT outsourcing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify super-cap covers: data breach, IP infringement, confidentiality breach, and wilful misconduct.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Indemnification obligations are one-way (customer indemnifies provider only)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: One-way indemnification leaves customer exposed to provider-caused third-party claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Mutual indemnification is standard. Provider indemnifies for IP infringement; customer for misuse.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with mutual indemnification. Provider: IP infringement, data breach. Customer: misuse, breach of use restrictions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Indemnification obligations are uncapped and outside general liability cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncapped indemnities create unlimited exposure which is commercially unreasonable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Indemnities typically subject to super-cap of 2-5x annual fees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Apply super-cap to indemnification. Require mitigation duties and sole conduct of defence.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual indemnification with provider covering IP/data breach and customer covering misuse
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Balanced mutual indemnification allocates risk to the party best able to manage it.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard market practice across enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: notice requirements, cooperation obligations, sole conduct of defence, and super-cap coverage.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No uptime or availability commitment in SaaS agreement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without uptime commitment, provider has no accountability for service availability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SaaS: 99.9% uptime is standard. Enterprise: 99.95% or higher.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require minimum 99.9% uptime with tiered service credits: 10% for <99.9%, 25% for <99.5%, termination right for <99.0%.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service credits capped below 10% of monthly fees
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Very low credit caps provide negligible incentive for provider to maintain service quality.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Service credits typically capped at 20-30% of monthly fees per month.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate cap to 25-30% of monthly fees. Add termination right if credits exceed cap for 3 consecutive months.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service credits stated as sole and exclusive remedy for SLA failures
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: If credits are the only remedy, customer has no recourse for repeated or severe failures.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Credits as primary remedy, but termination right preserved for persistent failure.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept credits as primary remedy but add: right to terminate for repeated SLA failures and preserve general liability claims.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: 99.9%+ uptime with tiered service credits up to 30% and termination right for persistent failure
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive SLA with meaningful financial remedies and ultimate exit right.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Best practice for enterprise SaaS and managed services.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: measurement methodology, exclusion reasonableness, credit request process, and reporting cadence.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No termination for convenience right for the customer
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without convenience termination, customer is locked in for full term regardless of business changes.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Most enterprise contracts include termination for convenience with 90-day notice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require termination for convenience with 90-day notice. Accept reasonable early termination fee if necessary.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Termination for convenience requires payment of 100% of remaining contract value
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Full remaining value penalty effectively negates the termination right.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Declining scale: year 1 100%, year 2 50%, year 3 25%. Or: 3-6 months fees maximum.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with declining fee: 50% year 1, 25% year 2, 0% year 3+. Or flat 3 months fees as breakage fee.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No right to terminate for material breach by the other party
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Termination for cause is a fundamental contractual right that should never be excluded.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Universal: right to terminate for material breach with cure period (typically 30 days).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Both parties must have right to terminate for material breach after 30-day cure period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual termination for cause (30-day cure) and convenience (90-day notice, reasonable fee)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Balanced termination rights protect both parties while maintaining flexibility.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard for most enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: transition assistance obligations, data return/deletion, and surviving clauses.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Confidentiality obligations are one-way (protecting discloser only)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: In most commercial relationships, both parties share confidential information.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Mutual confidentiality is standard unless clearly one-way disclosure (e.g., NDA for due diligence).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose mutual confidentiality unless relationship is genuinely one-way.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Confidentiality obligations end on contract termination (no survival period)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Information remains confidential regardless of contract status. No survival equals no protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 3-5 year survival period is standard. Trade secrets: indefinite.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require minimum 3-year survival period. Trade secrets protected indefinitely.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual confidentiality with standard exceptions and 3+ year survival period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive mutual confidentiality with reasonable survival is market standard.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard market practice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: definition scope, exceptions (public domain, independent development, prior knowledge), and return/destruction obligations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA does not provide controller with audit rights over processor
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 28(3)(h) mandates audit rights. Absence makes DPA non-compliant.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Audit rights are mandatory under GDPR. Can be satisfied by: on-site audit, remote audit, or third-party certification (SOC 2, ISO 27001).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Include audit rights. Offer compromise: accept SOC 2/ISO 27001 report as alternative to on-site audit, with escalation to on-site for cause.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA has no breach notification obligation or timeframe exceeds 72 hours
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Controllers must notify supervisory authority within 72 hours. Processor notification must be faster.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Processor notification within 24-48 hours is standard to allow controller time for assessment and authority notification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require notification within 24 hours with: nature of breach, data categories affected, estimated impact, and mitigation measures.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: General authorisation for sub-processors without meaningful objection right
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR allows general authorisation but requires opportunity to object to new sub-processors.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day advance notification with objection right and termination option if objection not resolved.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept general authorisation with: 30-day notification, objection right, and right to terminate if new sub-processor not acceptable.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA covers all Article 28(3) requirements with 24-48hr breach notification and audit rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive DPA meeting all GDPR requirements.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for GDPR-compliant DPA.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: sub-processor list, TOMs schedule, processing scope annex, and SCC module if international transfers.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: All IP created under contract remains with provider, customer gets licence only
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: For bespoke development funded by customer, provider retaining all IP is unreasonable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Bespoke work: customer owns foreground IP. Off-the-shelf: provider retains, customer gets licence.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: For bespoke deliverables: full IP assignment. For provider tools: non-exclusive licence. Define boundary clearly.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Customer demands assignment of all IP including provider's pre-existing tools and methodologies
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Assigning background IP would prevent provider from serving other clients.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Customer owns bespoke foreground IP. Provider retains background IP with licence to customer.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Assign bespoke foreground IP to customer. Retain background IP with non-exclusive licence. Define boundary in schedule.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Foreground IP assigned to customer with licence-back for general tools, background IP retained by provider with licence to customer
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Market-standard IP allocation balancing customer's investment with provider's business needs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard for enterprise software development and professional services.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: clear definition of foreground vs background, IP schedule, licence scope, and non-infringement warranty.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: 100% payment required upfront before any delivery
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Full prepayment eliminates all financial leverage for quality and delivery.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Milestone-based payments or monthly in arrears. Maximum 25-30% upfront deposit.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with milestone-based payment: 25% on signing, remainder on delivery milestones with acceptance criteria.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Payment terms exceed net-90 days
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Extended payment terms beyond 90 days create significant cash flow risk for providers.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Enterprise: net-30 or net-45. Maximum: net-60.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with net-30. Accept net-45 maximum with early payment discount incentive.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Auto-renewal with provider right to increase pricing without cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncapped auto-renewal price increases are essentially unilateral contract amendments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Price increases capped at CPI or 3-5% per annum with 60-90 day notice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept auto-renewal but cap increases at CPI or 5% (whichever lower) with 90-day notice and opt-out right.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Net-30 payment with clear invoicing process, late interest, and dispute mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Standard payment terms with clear process for both parties.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: invoice format requirements, PO process, disputed amount handling, and set-off rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: All warranties disclaimed including fitness for purpose
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Complete warranty disclaimer leaves buyer with no quality assurance for paid services.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Services: warranty of professional standards. Software: warranty of material conformity to specifications.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: warranty of conformity to specifications, professional standards for services, and defect correction period.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Warranty period less than 90 days for software deliverables
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Short warranty periods may not allow sufficient time to discover defects in normal use.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Software: 12 months. Professional services: 90 days. Hardware: 12-36 months.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate 12-month warranty for software, 90 days for services. Include defect correction obligation within warranty.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: 12-month warranty for software conformity to specifications with defect correction obligation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Standard software warranty providing reasonable assurance of quality.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for enterprise software.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: specification document referenced, defect severity classification, and response/fix times.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure clause explicitly excludes pandemic events
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Post-COVID exclusion is increasingly common but creates coverage gap for future events.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Evolving post-COVID. Some include pandemic with mitigation obligations; others exclude.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate: include pandemic but with enhanced mitigation obligations. Add 60-day review mechanism and termination right at 90 days.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure provides indefinite suspension without termination right
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Indefinite suspension traps parties in non-performing contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Termination right after 60-90 days of continued force majeure event.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require termination right if force majeure continues beyond 60 days, without penalty and with prorated refund.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure with defined events, mitigation obligations, and 60-90 day termination right
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Balanced force majeure protecting both parties with clear exit mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Best practice for enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: notification requirements, mitigation duty, payment during FM, and termination mechanics.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Governing law of jurisdiction unfamiliar to or unfavourable for the customer
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unfamiliar governing law increases legal costs and outcome uncertainty.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Governing law typically follows provider's jurisdiction or negotiated neutral venue.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose customer's home jurisdiction or mutually convenient neutral jurisdiction (e.g., England and Wales, New York).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Disputes resolved exclusively by litigation in foreign jurisdiction
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Foreign litigation is expensive and unpredictable, especially for enforcement.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Tiered: negotiation, mediation, then arbitration or litigation. Arbitration preferred for cross-border.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose tiered dispute resolution: 30-day negotiation, mediation, then ICC/LCIA arbitration in neutral venue.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Tiered dispute resolution: escalation, mediation, then arbitration with neutral venue
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Tiered approach reduces costs and preserves commercial relationships.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Best practice for cross-border enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: timeframes for each tier, arbitration rules (ICC/LCIA/SIAC), number of arbitrators, and language.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider can assign contract without customer consent
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unrestricted assignment allows provider to transfer obligations to unknown or inadequate party.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Assignment requires prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require prior written consent for assignment. Allow exception only for assignment to affiliate meeting same standards.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Assignment requires consent (not unreasonably withheld), with affiliate exception
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Standard assignment restriction balancing flexibility with protection.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: change of control treated as assignment, affiliate qualification requirements, and flow-down of obligations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No insurance requirements specified for provider
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without insurance, provider may lack financial backing for contractual obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Professional indemnity: 2x contract value. Cyber: EUR 2-5M. General liability: EUR 5M.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: professional indemnity (2x annual fees min), cyber insurance (EUR 2M min), general liability (EUR 5M).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Professional indemnity, cyber, and general liability insurance at market levels maintained throughout term
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive insurance backing provides financial assurance for claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard market practice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: certificate of insurance provided annually, notification of material changes, and additional insured status.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No audit rights for customer over provider's performance or compliance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without audit rights, compliance claims and cost accuracy are unverifiable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual audit right with 30-day notice. Cost borne by auditing party unless material issues found.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require annual audit right covering: security, compliance, financial accuracy. ISO 27001/SOC 2 reports as baseline; on-site for cause.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Annual audit right with 30-day notice, cost allocation based on findings, and SOC 2/ISO 27001 report as baseline
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Pragmatic audit rights balancing oversight with operational disruption concerns.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: scope includes sub-processors, remediation timelines for findings, and confidentiality of audit results.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Non-solicitation period exceeds 24 months or covers all employees (not just project team)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive non-solicitation restricts talent mobility disproportionately.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 12 months, limited to employees directly involved in the engagement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with: 12-month period, limited to individuals directly involved in the engagement, and mutual application.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Mutual 12-month non-solicitation limited to directly involved personnel
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Reasonable non-solicitation protecting both parties' workforce investments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard for professional services and outsourcing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: applies to solicitation only (not general recruitment), mutual application, and reasonable scope.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Entire agreement clause with no carve-out for fraudulent misrepresentation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Some jurisdictions require fraud carve-out to maintain clause enforceability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Include fraud carve-out for enforceability in English law and many EU jurisdictions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Add carve-out: 'Nothing in this clause shall limit liability for fraudulent misrepresentation.'

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider can increase prices without any cap or limitation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncapped price increases give provider unilateral power to change contract economics.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual increases capped at CPI or 3-5%. Benchmarking rights after year 2-3.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Cap increases at lesser of CPI or 5% per annum. Require 90-day notice. Add benchmarking rights at year 3.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Auto-renewal with no reminder notice or very short opt-out window (<30 days)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Silent auto-renewal traps customers who miss the narrow opt-out window.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 60-90 day notice of renewal with opt-out right.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require 60-day advance notice of upcoming renewal with right to opt out up to 30 days before renewal date.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider can unilaterally change service specifications or terms
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unilateral change rights undermine the stability of contractual commitments.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Material changes require mutual agreement. Minor changes with notice and objection right.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Material changes: mutual written agreement. Minor: 30-day notice with objection right. Define materiality threshold.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider claims ownership of customer data or derived/aggregated data
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Customer data belongs to the customer. Provider claiming ownership is a significant risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Customer owns all customer data. Provider may use anonymised/aggregated data for service improvement with consent.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable: customer owns all customer data. Provider may use truly anonymised aggregate data only for service improvement, with express consent.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Data processing location not specified or 'worldwide' processing permitted
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unspecified data location creates GDPR transfer risk and data sovereignty concerns.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: EEA processing for EU data. Specific data centre locations identified.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require EEA data processing and storage. If non-EEA necessary, specific locations with appropriate transfer mechanisms.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Vague service description without clear boundaries on scope
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Ambiguous scope leads to disputes about what is included in the contract price.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Detailed service description as schedule with clear inclusions, exclusions, and change control process.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require detailed service description schedule. Define scope explicitly. Implement change control for any scope changes.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International data transfer with no transfer mechanism (SCCs, adequacy decision, BCRs)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Post-Schrems II, all EEA-to-third-country transfers require a valid transfer mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SCCs (Module 2 or 3) with TIA and supplementary measures. Or adequacy decision where available.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Implement SCCs with completed TIA. If US: verify Data Privacy Framework certification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No representation of compliance with applicable laws
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without compliance representation, no contractual recourse if provider violates applicable laws.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Mutual representation of compliance with all applicable laws.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require mutual representation and ongoing obligation to comply with all applicable laws, with notification of any material non-compliance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contractual limitation period shorter than statutory limitation period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Short limitation periods may prevent discovery and pursuit of legitimate claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Contractual limitation typically matches statutory period (6 years in England, varies by jurisdiction).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Align limitation period with applicable statutory period. Minimum 2 years from discovery for latent issues.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No remedy if deliverables infringe third-party IP rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without IP infringement remedy, customer bears all risk from third-party claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Provider must: defend claim, modify/replace to be non-infringing, or refund if unable to cure.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: defend and indemnify for IP claims, with replace/modify/refund obligation and IP indemnification outside general cap.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Critical software dependency without source code escrow arrangement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without escrow, vendor failure means permanent loss of access to critical system.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Escrow standard for business-critical bespoke software. Less common for SaaS (API escrow emerging).
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: For bespoke software: require source code escrow. For SaaS: require data portability and escrow of configurations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Financial entity ICT contract without DORA Article 30 required provisions
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: DORA mandates specific contractual provisions. Missing provisions create regulatory risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: All DORA Article 30 provisions must be included for financial entity ICT outsourcing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable for in-scope entities. Add DORA compliance schedule covering all Article 30 requirements.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Essential entity supply chain contract without NIS2 security requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: NIS2 requires supply chain security measures. Contracts must implement these.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: NIS2-specific security schedule required for essential/important entity supply chains.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Add NIS2 compliance schedule: vulnerability management, incident notification, patching obligations, access control.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No transition/exit assistance obligation in outsourcing agreement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without exit assistance, transition to replacement provider is extremely difficult and risky.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 6-12 month exit assistance at prevailing rates with knowledge transfer and data migration.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require 12-month exit assistance at prevailing rates: knowledge transfer, data migration, parallel running, documentation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: 12-month exit assistance at prevailing rates with knowledge transfer, data migration, and parallel running
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive exit assistance enabling orderly transition.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Best practice for outsourcing and managed services.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: key personnel retention during transition, documentation deliverables, and acceptance criteria.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider can subcontract without customer approval or notification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Unrestricted subcontracting dilutes accountability and introduces unknown risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Prior written approval for subcontracting, with flow-down of obligations.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require prior written approval. Flow-down all security, confidentiality, and data protection obligations.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Long-term contract (>3 years) without pricing benchmarking rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without benchmarking, pricing diverges from market over multi-year terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Benchmarking rights after year 2-3 with adjustment mechanism.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include annual benchmarking right from year 3. Adjustment mechanism if pricing exceeds market by >10%.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Minimum annual spend commitment without volume flexibility or rebaseline mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Inflexible commitments create take-or-pay risk if business volumes change.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: +/-20% volume band with annual rebaseline and material change carve-out.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Add +/-20% flexibility band, annual rebaseline mechanism, and carve-out for material business change (M&A, restructuring).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Critical service without customer step-in rights for provider failure
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without step-in, customer has no mechanism to maintain continuity during provider failure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Step-in rights for critical outsourcing, triggered by persistent SLA failure or insolvency.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include step-in rights: triggered by persistent material SLA failure or insolvency event. Duration: until service restored or transitioned.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Deliverable-based contract without acceptance testing process
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without acceptance testing, deliverables are deemed accepted regardless of quality.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day acceptance testing period with defined criteria and right to reject.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: 30-day acceptance testing, defined criteria, right to reject with specifics, 2 cure attempts, refund if uncured.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: 30-day acceptance testing with defined criteria, rejection rights, and cure period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Standard acceptance process protecting both parties.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Market standard for deliverable-based contracts.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: criteria are objective and measurable, cure period reasonable (typically 2 attempts), and final remedy clear.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No termination right or notification on change of control of provider
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Provider acquisition may fundamentally change service quality, strategy, or competitive position.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day notification of change of control with right to terminate within 90 days without penalty.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: 30-day advance notification of change of control, 90-day right to terminate without penalty.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider handling sensitive data without security certification (ISO 27001, SOC 2)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without third-party certification, security posture is unverified and self-assessed only.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: ISO 27001 or SOC 2 Type II certification for enterprise data handling.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require ISO 27001 or SOC 2 Type II. Accept roadmap for certification with interim audit rights and enhanced security schedule.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Combination of proprietary formats, no data portability, and high termination fees
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Multiple lock-in mechanisms create cumulative barrier to exit.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard formats, data portability, reasonable termination fees, and transition assistance.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Address each lock-in mechanism: require standard formats, API data export, declining termination fees, and 12-month transition assistance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Critical service without DR/BCP commitments or testing obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without DR commitments, service has no guaranteed recovery capability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: RPO: 1-4 hours. RTO: 4-8 hours. Annual DR testing with customer notification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: documented BCP/DR plan, RPO/RTO targets appropriate to criticality, annual testing, and results reporting.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No defined data retention or deletion policy
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Undefined retention creates GDPR storage limitation compliance risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Defined retention periods per data category with automatic deletion at expiry.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define retention periods for all data categories. Require automatic deletion at expiry and on-demand deletion within 30 days of request.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: EEA personal data transferred to non-adequate country without Standard Contractual Clauses
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without SCCs (or equivalent), the transfer violates GDPR Chapter V.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: EU SCCs (2021 version) with completed annexes and Transfer Impact Assessment.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Execute SCCs (appropriate module), conduct TIA, implement supplementary measures per EDPB guidance.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No representation of authority to enter into the agreement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without authority representation, contract may be voidable if signatory lacked authority.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard boilerplate: each party represents it has authority to enter into and perform the agreement.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include standard mutual representation of authority, due incorporation, and no conflicts.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap tied to most recent invoice or last month's fees rather than annual value
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Invoice-based cap creates trivially small liability cap, especially for month-to-month billing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Cap based on 12 months of fees or total contract value, not individual invoices.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Counter with cap based on 12 months of aggregate fees paid or payable, not individual invoice amounts.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Different liability caps for each party without commercial justification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Asymmetric caps indicate power imbalance that may not reflect actual risk allocation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Symmetrical caps for equivalent obligations. Asymmetry acceptable where risk profile genuinely differs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose symmetrical caps unless risk profile genuinely justifies asymmetry. Require clear rationale for any asymmetry.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Services or software provided 'as is' with all warranties disclaimed
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: 'As is' provision eliminates all quality commitments for paid services.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Never appropriate for paid enterprise services. Warranties of conformity and professional standards expected.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Reject 'as is'. Require minimum: warranty of material conformity to specifications and performance using reasonable skill and care.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Software development without open source policy or SBOM requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without open source governance, copyleft contamination or licence compliance risk is unmanaged.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: SBOM delivery, approved licence list, no copyleft without approval, and open source IP indemnification.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: SBOM (SPDX/CycloneDX format), approved licence list (no copyleft without approval), and open source indemnification.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Entire agreement clause with fraud carve-out and clear schedule hierarchy
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Proper entire agreement clause protects against pre-contractual representation claims.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard boilerplate for enterprise agreements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: all negotiated terms captured in agreement, schedule hierarchy clear, and all referenced documents listed.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International contract without sanctions compliance representation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Sanctions violations carry severe penalties including criminal liability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Representation of non-sanctioned status and ongoing screening obligation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require representation of non-sanctioned status, ongoing screening, notification of any sanctions development, and termination right for sanctions breach.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No list of clauses surviving termination or expiry
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without survival clause, obligations like confidentiality, IP, and indemnification may end on termination.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Explicit survival clause listing: confidentiality, IP, indemnification, limitation of liability, and audit rights.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include survival clause covering: confidentiality, IP, indemnification, limitation, audit rights, data protection, and dispute resolution.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liquidated damages that may be deemed a penalty under applicable law
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: In English law, penalty clauses are unenforceable. Liquidated damages must be genuine pre-estimate.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Liquidated damages at 0.5-2% per week, capped at 10-20%, as genuine pre-estimate of loss.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Ensure liquidated damages are: genuine pre-estimate of likely loss, proportionate to breach, and properly documented.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Service credits require customer request within short window (e.g., 7 days)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Short request windows mean many valid credits go unclaimed.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Automatic application or 30-60 day request window.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Negotiate: automatic application of credits or 60-day request window. Credits should not require manual claim.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Governing law of customer's jurisdiction or mutually agreed neutral jurisdiction
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Familiar governing law reduces legal costs and increases certainty.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Governing law of one party's jurisdiction (typically provider) or neutral venue for cross-border.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: consistent with dispute resolution venue and enforceable in both parties' jurisdictions.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No waiver clause (failure to exercise right does not waive it)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without waiver clause, inaction on a breach may be interpreted as waiving rights.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard boilerplate: no waiver except in writing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include standard no-waiver clause: failure to exercise right is not waiver; waiver must be in writing.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No severability clause
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without severability, an unenforceable provision could invalidate the entire agreement.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard boilerplate: unenforceable provisions severed without affecting remainder.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include standard severability clause.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No formal notice provisions (addresses, methods, deemed delivery)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without notice provisions, important communications may not be properly served.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Formal notice clause with: addresses, permitted methods (email and post), and deemed delivery rules.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include notice clause with: named recipients, dual delivery (email and post), and deemed delivery times.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No requirement for amendments to be in writing signed by authorised representatives
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without written amendment requirement, contract terms may be informally varied.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Amendments in writing signed by authorised representatives of both parties.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: amendments only by written agreement signed by authorised representatives of both parties.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Agreement includes counterparts clause allowing separate execution
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Counterparts clause enables practical execution when parties are in different locations.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard boilerplate, increasingly including electronic signature.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Consider adding express acceptance of electronic signatures for practical execution.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap is per-incident only with no aggregate cap
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Per-incident cap without aggregate means cumulative claims can exceed planned exposure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Both per-incident and aggregate caps. Aggregate typically 12 months fees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require both per-incident and aggregate annual cap. Per-incident: 50% of aggregate. Aggregate: 12 months fees.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Broad licence-back to provider covering customer-specific IP
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Licence-back should cover general tools and methodologies, not customer-specific deliverables.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Non-exclusive licence-back for general methodologies and tools only, excluding customer-specific IP.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Limit licence-back to: general tools, methodologies, and non-proprietary techniques. Exclude all customer-specific IP.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Agreement excludes third-party rights (Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 or equivalent)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excluding third-party rights prevents unintended beneficiaries from enforcing contract terms.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Standard exclusion in English law agreements. Similar exclusions in other jurisdictions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: any intentional third-party beneficiaries are explicitly carved out.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No restrictions on press releases, case studies, or marketing referencing the relationship
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Uncontrolled publicity can cause reputational damage or reveal competitive intelligence.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Prior written approval for any publicity referencing the relationship.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include clause requiring mutual prior written approval for any press releases, case studies, or marketing materials.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No anti-corruption or anti-bribery provisions in international contract
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: UK Bribery Act and FCPA have extraterritorial reach. Contractual protections reduce exposure.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Anti-bribery representation and warranty with compliance programme requirement and termination right for breach.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include anti-bribery clause: representation of compliance, adequate procedures, audit rights, and immediate termination for breach.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: DPA does not require data deletion or return on termination
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: GDPR Article 28(3)(g) requires deletion or return of personal data. Non-compliant without this.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Return or deletion within 30 days with written certification of destruction.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Require return or deletion within 30 days of contract end, with written certification and destruction of all copies.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SLA targets defined but measurement methodology not specified
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without agreed measurement methodology, SLA compliance is contested.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Measurement methodology defined including: tools, measurement points, exclusions, and reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define: measurement tool, calculation formula, measurement points, planned downtime exclusions, and monthly reporting format.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap applies to wilful misconduct and gross negligence
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Capping liability for intentional wrongdoing removes deterrence and may be unenforceable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Wilful misconduct and gross negligence carved out from liability cap in most jurisdictions.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Carve out wilful misconduct and gross negligence from liability cap. These should be uncapped or subject to highest super-cap.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No specific indemnification for data breach caused by provider
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Data breaches can result in significant regulatory fines and remediation costs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Data breach indemnification covering: regulatory fines (where insurable), notification costs, credit monitoring, and legal fees.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include data breach indemnification covering: notification costs, credit monitoring, legal fees, and regulatory fines (where legally permissible).

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Audit rights limited to customer's own personnel (no third-party auditor)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Effective audits often require specialist third-party auditors.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Audit by customer or qualified third party (subject to confidentiality), with SOC 2 report as baseline alternative.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Permit audit by customer or qualified third-party auditor bound by NDA. Accept SOC 2 Type II as alternative to routine audits.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Termination effective immediately with no wind-down or transition period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Immediate termination without wind-down causes abrupt service discontinuity.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Wind-down period of 30-90 days for standard services; 6-12 months for complex outsourcing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include wind-down period: minimum 90 days with obligation to maintain service delivery during transition.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No process for disputing invoiced amounts
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without dispute process, billing disagreements may trigger service suspension.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day dispute window, undisputed amounts paid, continued service during dispute resolution.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include: 30-day dispute window, payment of undisputed amounts, no service suspension during dispute, good-faith resolution.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Force majeure clause excludes cyber attacks or is silent on them
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Cyber attacks are increasingly common but their FM status is legally contested.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Evolving. Some include cyber attacks as FM; others exclude. Key: provider should mitigate through security investment.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Exclude provider-targeted cyber attacks from FM (provider should have adequate defences). Include only nation-state level attacks beyond reasonable mitigation.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No obligation to continue service delivery during termination notice period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Service cessation during notice period defeats the purpose of the notice period.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Obligation to continue service at contracted levels throughout notice and transition period.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include express obligation to maintain service at contracted levels throughout notice period and transition.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Indemnification without mitigation duties for the indemnified party
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without mitigation duties, indemnified party has no incentive to minimise losses.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Indemnified party must: notify promptly, allow indemnifier to conduct defence, and cooperate with mitigation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Include standard indemnification procedure: prompt notification, indemnifier right to conduct defence, cooperation obligation, and mitigation duty.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Planned maintenance window excluded from SLA calculation exceeds 8 hours per month
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive maintenance windows effectively reduce the real uptime commitment.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 4-8 hours planned maintenance per month, outside business hours, with 5 business days notice.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Cap planned maintenance at 8 hours/month outside business hours. Require 5 business days notice. Excess counts against SLA.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: All improvements to deliverables automatically vest in provider
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Customer-funded improvements vesting in provider is unreasonable for bespoke work.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Customer-funded improvements belong to customer. Provider-funded improvements negotiable.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Customer-funded improvements: customer ownership. Provider-funded improvements: provider ownership with licence to customer.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider right to suspend service for late payment without cure period
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Immediate suspension for late payment is disproportionate and risks business disruption.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Suspension right after 30-day cure period following written notice of overdue payment.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept suspension right but require: written notice, 30-day cure period, and no suspension of critical services affecting third parties.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Broad residuals clause allowing use of 'retained in memory' information
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Broad residuals clauses can effectively negate confidentiality for non-documented information.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Residuals clauses are provider-friendly. If accepted, limit to: general ideas and concepts, not specific data or designs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: If accepting residuals: limit to general skills and know-how. Exclude: specific designs, customer data, trade secrets, and personal data.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: ICT contract includes all DORA Article 30 provisions with exit strategy and testing rights
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Comprehensive DORA compliance for financial entity ICT outsourcing.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Required by regulation for in-scope entities.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: service description detail, data location specificity, security testing scope, and exit strategy feasibility.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Supply chain contract meets NIS2 Article 21 security requirements with incident notification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: NIS2-compliant supply chain security provisions.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Required by regulation for essential/important entity supply chains.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: vulnerability management specifics, patching SLAs, incident notification timelines, and audit rights.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Indemnified party has no right to participate in defence of claim
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Complete exclusion from defence may result in poor settlement decisions affecting indemnified party.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Indemnifier controls defence. Indemnified party: right to participate at own cost, consent for settlement affecting them.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Indemnifier controls defence. Add: indemnified party right to participate (own cost), consent required for settlement involving admission or injunction.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Customer demands unlimited or very frequent audit rights (e.g., quarterly on-site)
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive audit frequency disrupts operations and is disproportionate to risk.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual audit right with additional for-cause audits. SOC 2 report as baseline.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Offer: annual planned audit with 30 days notice, plus for-cause audits. SOC 2 Type II report provided annually as alternative.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI service terms allow use of customer data for model training without opt-out
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Using customer data for AI training without consent is a GDPR and confidentiality violation.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Explicit opt-out from AI training is increasingly standard. Best practice: opt-in only.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Non-negotiable. Require explicit prohibition on using customer data for model training. Opt-in only with separate consent.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: AI-generated outputs have unclear IP ownership allocation
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: AI-generated content may not be copyrightable in some jurisdictions, creating IP uncertainty.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Evolving. Customer typically owns outputs generated from their data. Provider retains model IP.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Define: customer owns outputs generated from customer data. Provider retains model IP. Include IP warranty for AI outputs.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: No contractual backup frequency or restore testing commitment
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without backup guarantees, data recovery capability is unknown and untested.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Daily backups, 90-day retention, quarterly restore testing.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: daily backups, 90-day retention, quarterly restore test, and notification of test results.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Warranty limited to 'reasonable endeavours' rather than fitness for purpose
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Reasonable endeavours is a low standard that may excuse failed delivery if effort was made.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Services: reasonable skill and care. Software: material conformity to specifications.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: For services: reasonable skill and care consistent with industry standards. For software: material conformity to specifications.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Complex engagement without key person clause for critical roles
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Service quality often depends on specific individuals. Their removal disrupts delivery.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Named key personnel, 30-day notice of changes, customer approval for replacements.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Name key personnel in schedule. Require 30-day notice of departure, customer approval for replacements, and knowledge transfer.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Provider right to immediately suspend service for any payment dispute
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Immediate suspension for disputed amounts is disproportionate and coercive.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: 30-day cure period, no suspension while amounts genuinely disputed, no suspension of critical services.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require: written notice, 30-day cure period, no suspension while amounts are genuinely disputed in good faith.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: SLA defined but no reporting obligation to evidence compliance
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Without reporting, SLA compliance is self-declared and unverifiable.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Monthly SLA report with: metrics, calculations, exclusions applied, and trend analysis.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Require monthly SLA report within 10 business days. Include: raw data, calculation methodology, exclusions applied, and trend.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Long-term contract includes continuous improvement and innovation obligations
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Innovation clause ensures service evolves with technology and business needs.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Annual innovation roadmap and continuous improvement targets for contracts >3 years.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: innovation is funded, roadmap is jointly agreed, and improvements don't create lock-in.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Multiple contracts with same provider but no umbrella or framework agreement
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Multiple standalone contracts create inconsistent terms and fragmented liability.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Framework/umbrella agreement governing all engagements with consistent liability and general terms.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose MSA/framework agreement with consistent liability, confidentiality, and general terms. Individual SOWs for specific services.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Contract includes environmental sustainability and carbon reporting requirements
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: CSRD and ESG requirements increasingly require supply chain sustainability reporting.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Emerging. Leading contracts include: carbon reporting, energy efficiency, and sustainability targets.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept. Verify: reporting format (GHG Protocol), frequency (annual), and targets are achievable and measurable.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: Liability cap exceeds 5x annual contract value without commercial justification
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Excessive caps disproportionate to fees create unsustainable risk for providers.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Base cap: 1-2x annual fees. Super-cap: 2-5x for specific carve-outs.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Propose base cap at 12-24 months fees with super-cap at 2-3x for data breach and IP infringement.

negotiation_intelligence.condition: International transfer relying solely on adequacy decision without backup mechanism
negotiation_intelligence.explanation: Adequacy decisions can be invalidated (Schrems I, Schrems II). Backup mechanism recommended.
negotiation_intelligence.market_standard: Adequacy decision as primary mechanism with SCCs as backup in case of invalidation.
negotiation_intelligence.suggested_response: Accept adequacy decision as primary. Include SCCs as fall-back mechanism in case adequacy decision is revoked.
